Artificial Intelligence

AI

Productivity

Technology

Tech

It runs on a schedule, lives in Telegram, and never waits to be asked

9 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Source: Image Generated by GPT 5.5
At 11:22 this morning, my phone buzzed with a Telegram message I had not asked for. It included 5 Apple and tech stories from the last 24 hours, and the current weather in my location.
What’s interesting here is that I didn't open a browser or type a prompt into an AI app to get this. I had already told an AI what I wanted in one sentence the day before, and then it just did the thing.
This was all made possible by an AI assistant called MyClaw. Let me tell you how I set it up, what it helped me with, what you can use it for, and, most importantly, how it can help you be more productive.
This is not another chatbot, and that distinction matters
MyClaw is not just a place to chat with an AI. It is a managed agent workspace. You hand it a goal, a briefing, a research summary, an alert, and a recurring task, and it does the…
